frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Kal jo case aaya news mein, bura toh bahut hai, lekin itni raat ko akeli kyun nikli thi?
B: Haan, zamaana theek nahi hai aajkal.
A: Bas wahi, main toh ghar ki ladkiyon ko bhi bolta hoon, apna khayal rakho, late night bahar mat raho. Practical baat hai, duniya ko hum ek din mein thodi badal denge.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it shifts responsibility for violence onto women's behavior and mobility rather than condemning male perpetrators. It uses protective, practical-sounding advice and victim-questioning ('itni raat ko akeli kyun nikli thi?') characteristic of this frame.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 51. A  factory owner in Surat who employs around 80 workers mostly from his own community. You are talking to your accountant over chai after reading a news article about a proposed law making menstrual leave mandatory for all private companies. You are commenting on what this will mean for small businesses and why you think the government does not understand how business works. Your concern is also that most of your workers are from the same community and you feel pressure to maintain a certain kind of workplace culture where such things are not openly discussed.
